resposta:
solution: ```python
class Solution:
    def reorderedPowerOf2(self, n: int) -> bool:
        from collections import Counter
        target = Counter(str(n))
        for p in range(30):
            if Counter(str(1 << p)) == target:
                return True
        return False
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Pre-compute digit-frequency signatures for all 30 possible 32-bit powers of two (up to 10^9). Compare the digit-frequency signature of n against each; if any match, a valid reordering exists.
